metadata.description: Tested causal edges for the No-Man causal risk intelligence system. Immutable except through governed process. See knowledge/governance.json.

edges.local_weight_note: Effect is most acute when NPLs concentrate in distressed sectors (real estate, construction) where collateral values simultaneously decline, as documented during the 1990s Japanese banking crisis
edges.name_en: Non-performing loan ratio → Bank capital adequacy ratio
edges.name_ja: 不良債権比率 → 銀行自己資本比率

edges.local_weight_note: Capital constraint on lending is especially binding for regional banks approaching regulatory minimums; bank-of-Japan credit supply surveys confirm capital ratio as a primary loan supply determinant
edges.name_en: Bank capital adequacy ratio → Bank loan volume
edges.name_ja: 銀行自己資本比率 → 銀行貸出量
edges.cycle_risk_note: Loan volume expansion supports regional economic activity; economic downturns from credit contraction can worsen NPL ratios and compress capital ratios, creating a self-reinforcing dynamic not captured in this DAG. This amplification mechanism was documented during the 1990s-2000s Japanese banking crisis.

edges.local_weight_note: High overhead costs (personnel, branch network) compress net operating profit and reduce retained earnings available to build capital; FSA has flagged OHR as a sustainability indicator for regional banks
edges.name_en: Bank operating cost ratio → Bank capital adequacy ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行経費率（OHR） → 銀行自己資本比率

edges.local_weight_note: NIM has been historically compressed for Japanese regional banks under ZIRP/NIRP; even small NIM improvements materially affect capital generation given thin margins
edges.name_en: Bank net interest margin → Bank capital adequacy ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行純利鞘 → 銀行自己資本比率

edges.monotone_note: Under normal conditions, securities holdings (especially equity) contribute to regulatory capital through unrealized gains. However, under rising interest rates, large JGB/local bond portfolios generate unrealized losses that compress capital ratios — reversing the sign of this edge. The relationship is highly regime-dependent.
edges.regime_condition: Sign holds positive under stable or falling interest rate environment. Under BoJ rate normalization (2024 onward), large fixed-income portfolios may generate unrealized losses, reversing sign to negative.
edges.regime_flip: Under rapid interest rate normalization, regional banks with large JGB/local bond portfolios may experience unrealized capital losses that compress capital ratios below the positive baseline; the direction of effect can become negative for banks with high bond duration exposure.
edges.local_weight_note: Regional banks hold disproportionately large JGB and local government bond portfolios as alternatives to low-margin regional lending; duration risk is concentrated in longer-maturity bonds acquired during the ZIRP era
edges.name_en: Bank securities portfolio → Bank capital adequacy ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行有価証券ポートフォリオ → 銀行自己資本比率

edges.local_weight_note: Effect is amplified in rural prefectures with few alternative lenders (no major bank branches, limited shinkin capacity); SMEs in such areas have no substitute for regional bank credit
edges.name_en: Bank loan volume → Regional SME credit access
edges.name_ja: 銀行貸出量 → 地域中小企業の信用アクセス

edges.local_weight_note: Effect is stronger when banks approach regulatory minimum capital thresholds; also stronger when NPLs require large provisioning that consumes retained earnings; documented extensively in the 1997-2002 Japanese banking crisis
edges.name_en: Bank non-performing loan ratio → Bank loan volume
edges.name_ja: 銀行不良債権比率 → 銀行貸出量
edges.cycle_risk_note: Credit contraction from high NPLs can suppress regional economic activity, increasing business failures and generating additional NPLs — a documented amplification mechanism in the 1990s Japanese banking crisis known as the 'credit crunch.' This feedback loop is not captured in this DAG.

edges.local_weight_note: Japanese regional banks rely primarily on local deposits for funding; wholesale funding access is limited for smaller regional banks, making the deposit base a binding constraint on lending capacity
edges.name_en: Local deposit base → Bank loan volume
edges.name_ja: 地域預金基盤 → 銀行貸出量
edges.cycle_risk_note: Loan volume expansion supports regional economic activity, which can sustain or grow the deposit base; conversely, deposit outflows from reduced confidence can further compress lending capacity. This bidirectional dynamic is not captured in this DAG.

edges.local_weight_note: PCA risk causes banks to shrink risk-weighted assets by cutting lending, even before formal PCA is imposed; anticipatory deleveraging can be substantial
edges.name_en: FSA prompt corrective action risk → Bank loan volume
edges.name_ja: 金融庁早期是正措置リスク → 銀行貸出量

edges.monotone_note: Under normal positive interest rate environments, higher policy rates widen NIM because deposit repricing lags loan repricing, allowing banks to earn higher spreads. However, under the zero lower bound (ZIRP/NIRP, Japan 2010-2023), deposit rates cannot fall below zero while loan rates are compressed, suppressing this mechanism. The sign is positive only in rate-normalization regimes.
edges.regime_condition: BoJ policy rate normalized above zero lower bound. Positive effect on NIM materializes only when rates are meaningfully above zero and banks can competitively reprice both assets and liabilities.
edges.regime_flip: Under ZIRP/NIRP, higher nominal policy rate has muted or zero effect on NIM because deposit rates are floored at zero; NIM was structurally compressed throughout the 2010-2023 period despite multiple BoJ guidance changes.
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese regional banks are particularly exposed to NIM compression under ZIRP because they cannot diversify into non-interest income as readily as major banks; rate normalization since 2024 is expected to improve NIM for well-positioned regional banks
edges.name_en: Bank of Japan policy rate → Bank net interest margin
edges.name_ja: 日本銀行政策金利 → 銀行純利鞘

edges.local_weight_note: Competition from major banks and shinkin is most intense in urban prefectural capitals where branch density is high; in isolated rural areas, regional banks may retain pricing power due to lack of alternatives
edges.name_en: Regional credit market competition → Bank net interest margin
edges.name_ja: 地域信用市場の競争 → 銀行純利鞘

edges.research_gap_note: Direct empirical evidence linking NPL levels to fee income suppression in Japanese regional banks is limited. The negative relationship is inferred through two mechanisms: (1) management attention diverted to NPL workout reduces cross-selling capacity, and (2) NPL-affected borrower relationships deteriorate, reducing ancillary fee opportunities. Systematic quantitative tests are lacking.
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese regional bank fee income is concentrated in investment product distribution (投資信託) and insurance agency (保険代理店), both of which require stable client relationships undermined by credit distress
edges.name_en: Bank non-performing loan ratio → Bank fee and commission income
edges.name_ja: 銀行不良債権比率 → 銀行役務取引等収益

edges.local_weight_note: FSA supervisory intensity in Japan includes informal guidance (行政指導, gyosei shido) which amplifies the formal examination channel; high NPL ratios trigger both formal on-site examination and informal supervisory engagement
edges.name_en: Bank non-performing loan ratio → FSA supervisory scrutiny
edges.name_ja: 銀行不良債権比率 → 金融庁の監督審査

edges.local_weight_note: Banks with capital ratios comfortably above thresholds receive less intensive FSA supervision; the FSA uses capital ratio as a primary early-warning indicator for allocating examination resources
edges.name_en: Bank capital adequacy ratio → FSA supervisory scrutiny
edges.name_ja: 銀行自己資本比率 → 金融庁の監督審査

edges.local_weight_note: FSA supervisory pressure in Japan manifests through both formal written guidance and informal administrative guidance (行政指導); banks receiving intensive monitoring face implicit pressure to improve capital ratios or reduce risk-weighted assets
edges.name_en: FSA supervisory scrutiny → Bank regulatory capital pressure
edges.name_ja: 金融庁の監督審査 → 銀行への規制資本圧力

edges.local_weight_note: Sustained regulatory capital pressure that is not resolved through capital raising or asset reduction eventually escalates into formal PCA risk; the progression from informal pressure to formal PCA is a documented feature of Japanese banking supervision
edges.name_en: Bank regulatory capital pressure → FSA prompt corrective action risk
edges.name_ja: 銀行への規制資本圧力 → 金融庁早期是正措置リスク

edges.research_gap_note: Systematic causal evidence on board governance quality and operating cost efficiency in Japanese regional banks is limited. The negative relationship is theoretically grounded (strong governance → incentive for cost discipline) and supported by international banking governance literature, but Japanese regional bank-specific quantitative tests are absent. Suggested test: panel regression of OHR on governance quality index (board independence, female director ratio) using FSA disclosure data.
international_evidence: Berger et al. 2005 (international bank efficiency and governance)
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese regional bank governance has historically been weak by international standards; many banks have high proportions of insiders on boards; FSA corporate governance reforms since 2015 target this channel
edges.name_en: Bank governance quality → Bank operating cost ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行ガバナンスの質 → 銀行経費率（OHR）

international_evidence: BCBS 2019 evaluation of Basel III implementation costs
European Banking Authority 2016 compliance cost study
edges.local_weight_note: Compliance cost burden is relatively heavier for smaller regional banks with limited compliance and IT staffing; major banks can spread fixed compliance costs over larger balance sheets
edges.name_en: Basel regulatory compliance burden → Bank operating cost ratio
edges.name_ja: バーゼル規制遵守負担 → 銀行経費率（OHR）

edges.local_weight_note: Physical branch presence is central to the main bank relationship in rural Japan where borrowers have limited digital banking access and value face-to-face contact with loan officers; branch closure signals reduced commitment to the relationship
edges.name_en: Bank branch network → Main bank relationship trust
edges.name_ja: 銀行支店ネットワーク → メインバンク関係の信頼

edges.local_weight_note: Branch closure reduces the bank's soft-information gathering capability, which is critical for SME lending in information-opaque regional markets; loan officers embedded in communities generate relationship-specific information that enables credit to informationally-captured borrowers
edges.name_en: Bank branch network → Regional SME credit access
edges.name_ja: 銀行支店ネットワーク → 地域中小企業の信用アクセス

edges.local_weight_note: Personnel and real estate costs of branch network are disproportionately large for regional banks; maintaining full-service branches in depopulating areas creates a fixed cost structure that cannot easily be reduced
edges.name_en: Bank branch network → Bank operating cost ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行支店ネットワーク → 銀行経費率（OHR）

edges.local_weight_note: In Japanese regional banking, the main bank relationship provides implicit insurance value (rescue lending commitment) that supports SME risk-taking and investment confidence; erosion of this trust increases precautionary cash holdings and reduces investment
edges.name_en: Main bank relationship trust → Borrower business confidence
edges.name_ja: メインバンク関係の信頼 → 借り手企業の景況感

edges.name_en: Bank regional reputation → Main bank relationship trust
edges.name_ja: 銀行の地域評判 → メインバンク関係の信頼

edges.name_en: Bank regional reputation → Depositor confidence
edges.name_ja: 銀行の地域評判 → 預金者の信頼

edges.local_weight_note: In small regional communities, bank financial distress signals are widely observed and discussed; NPL disclosures (mandated by FSA) directly affect community perception of the bank's stability
edges.name_en: Bank non-performing loan ratio → Bank regional reputation
edges.name_ja: 銀行不良債権比率 → 銀行の地域評判

edges.name_en: Depositor confidence → Local deposit base
edges.name_ja: 預金者の信頼 → 地域預金基盤
edges.cycle_risk_note: Deposit base supports lending which sustains regional economic activity; adverse economic outcomes from credit contraction can undermine depositor confidence, creating a potential adverse feedback loop not captured in this DAG.

edges.local_weight_note: Uncertainty aversion documented in Japanese SME investment behavior; confidence in bank relationships reduces precautionary cash accumulation and unlocks latent investment demand
edges.name_en: Borrower business confidence → Regional SME capital investment
edges.name_ja: 借り手企業の景況感 → 地域中小企業の設備投資

edges.local_weight_note: Japanese SMEs have limited alternative financing (stock market, bond market); bank credit is the primary enabler of capital investment; credit rationing directly constrains investment even for SMEs with positive NPV projects
edges.name_en: Regional SME credit access → Regional SME capital investment
edges.name_ja: 地域中小企業の信用アクセス → 地域中小企業の設備投資

edges.local_weight_note: SMEs account for approximately 70% of employment in Japanese prefectural economies; their investment decisions are the primary driver of regional labor demand fluctuations
edges.name_en: Regional SME capital investment → Regional employment
edges.name_ja: 地域中小企業の設備投資 → 地域雇用

edges.name_en: Regional employment → Regional GDP
edges.name_ja: 地域雇用 → 地域GDP

edges.local_weight_note: Access to startup credit is disproportionately important in regions with few VCs or alternative capital sources; regional bank credit availability directly conditions the feasibility of new business formation
edges.name_en: Regional SME credit access → Regional entrepreneurial activity
edges.name_ja: 地域中小企業の信用アクセス → 地域の起業活動

edges.name_en: Regional entrepreneurial activity → Regional employment
edges.name_ja: 地域の起業活動 → 地域雇用

edges.research_gap_note: Regional-level housing market to GDP linkage in Japan varies substantially across prefectures and is not well-established for depopulating rural regions. The positive relationship is strong in metropolitan areas but weak or ambiguous in regions with persistent population decline where housing demand is structurally falling. Suggested test: prefecture-level panel regression of GDP growth on housing starts, controlling for population trend.
edges.local_weight_note: Effect is concentrated in growing metropolitan prefectures; in depopulating prefectures, housing construction may not translate into sustained economic activity given falling demand fundamentals
edges.name_en: Regional housing market activity → Regional GDP
edges.name_ja: 地域住宅市場の活況 → 地域GDP

edges.local_weight_note: Population decline (人口減少) is the primary structural driver of deposit base erosion in rural Japan; over 30 prefectures face persistent outmigration of working-age population to urban centers; deposit balances track population levels with high correlation in regional bank data
edges.name_en: Regional population trend → Local deposit base
edges.name_ja: 地域人口動態 → 地域預金基盤

edges.local_weight_note: Population decline is among the most powerful structural drags on regional GDP in Japan; shrinking labor force and consumer base reduces both supply (labor) and demand (consumption) components of regional GDP
edges.name_en: Regional population trend → Regional GDP
edges.name_ja: 地域人口動態 → 地域GDP

edges.local_weight_note: Both labor supply (working-age population) and labor demand (consumer base) fall with population decline; employment level tracks population with a lag reflecting labor market adjustment
edges.name_en: Regional population trend → Regional employment
edges.name_ja: 地域人口動態 → 地域雇用

edges.regime_condition: Positive effect on deposit base materializes only when BoJ policy rate is meaningfully above zero; under ZIRP/NIRP, the mechanism (higher deposit rates attracting funds from alternative instruments) is suppressed.
edges.regime_flip: Under ZIRP/NIRP (2010-2024), higher nominal rates had minimal deposit-attracting effect because deposit rates remained near zero regardless of policy rate; the mechanism activates under rate normalization.
edges.local_weight_note: Rate normalization may trigger competition for deposits among regional banks and between banks and post office savings (Japan Post Bank), complicating the aggregate deposit-attracting effect
edges.name_en: Bank of Japan policy rate → Local deposit base
edges.name_ja: 日本銀行政策金利 → 地域預金基盤

edges.local_weight_note: Mortgage lending and real estate developer lending are significant components of regional bank loan books; tightening credit reduces both buyer purchasing power and developer construction activity
edges.name_en: Bank loan volume → Regional housing market activity
edges.name_ja: 銀行貸出量 → 地域住宅市場の活況

edges.local_weight_note: Local government spending (including healthcare, education, infrastructure, and public works) constitutes 30-40% of regional economic activity in many Japanese prefectures; fiscal consolidation in the face of population decline creates direct GDP headwinds
edges.name_en: Local government fiscal health → Regional GDP
edges.name_ja: 地方自治体の財政健全性 → 地域GDP

edges.local_weight_note: Regional banks are primary underwriters and holders of local government bonds (地方債); their continued portfolio commitment supports local government funding costs and issuance capacity; reduction in bank appetite for local bonds raises borrowing costs for municipalities
edges.name_en: Bank securities portfolio → Local government fiscal health
edges.name_ja: 銀行有価証券ポートフォリオ → 地方自治体の財政健全性
edges.cycle_risk_note: Local government fiscal health affects regional economic activity and household incomes, which in turn affect deposit levels and the bank's capacity to hold local government bonds. This mutual dependence between bank health and local government finance is not captured in this DAG.

edges.research_gap_note: Systematic causal evidence on board governance quality and NPL outcomes in Japanese regional banks is limited. The negative relationship is theoretically grounded (strong governance → better credit risk management → lower NPLs) and supported by international banking studies, but Japan-specific panel estimates are absent. Suggested test: panel regression of NPL ratio change on FSA governance quality assessment scores or Nikkei governance index, controlling for economic conditions.
international_evidence: Berger et al. 2005 (bank governance and risk, international panel)
IMF 2017 (bank corporate governance and performance in Asia)
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese regional bank governance has historically been weak by international standards; insider-dominated boards with limited independent oversight may tolerate evergreening (ever-greening) of non-performing loans, masking true NPL levels
edges.name_en: Bank governance quality → Bank non-performing loan ratio
edges.name_ja: 銀行ガバナンスの質 → 銀行不良債権比率

edges.local_weight_note: In concentrated regional credit markets with few lenders, SMEs face limited alternatives and reduced bargaining power; bank exit from a market reduces competitive pressure and worsens credit access for informationally opaque SMEs who cannot switch to distant banks. Effect is strongest in prefectures with one or two dominant regional banks.
edges.name_en: Regional credit market competition → Regional SME credit access
edges.name_ja: 地域信用市場の競争 → 地域中小企業の信用アクセス

edges.research_gap_note: Direct quantitative evidence linking regional bank NIM levels to SME business confidence in Japan is limited. The negative relationship is inferred through two plausible channels: (1) higher lending margins raise borrowing costs, suppressing investment willingness; (2) rising margins signal credit market tightening, increasing business uncertainty. Suggested test: cross-prefecture panel regression of SME business confidence index (BoJ Tankan regional component) on regional bank NIM, controlling for BoJ policy rate and regional economic conditions.
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese SMEs are highly bank-dependent with limited access to capital markets; NIM changes at their relationship bank directly affect their effective cost of borrowing. Under BoJ rate normalization since 2024, NIM widening at regional banks may suppress confidence in rate-sensitive sectors such as construction and retail
edges.name_en: Bank net interest margin → Borrower business confidence
edges.name_ja: 銀行純利鞘 → 借り手企業の景況感

edges.research_gap_note: The causal channel from bank NIM to SME credit access in Japanese regional banks requires more systematic testing. The negative sign assumes higher margins reflect higher loan pricing, reducing SME demand for credit. An alternative reading is that higher NIM reflects improved bank health enabling greater credit supply, which could reverse the sign. Suggested test: compare SME loan approval rates across prefectures with different regional bank NIM levels, controlling for macroeconomic conditions and bank-specific capital ratios.
edges.local_weight_note: Japanese SMEs have limited alternative financing; even modest increases in loan pricing can push marginal borrowers below the feasibility threshold for investment projects. Effect is strongest for informationally opaque SMEs in services and retail sectors without tangible collateral
edges.name_en: Bank net interest margin → Regional SME credit access
edges.name_ja: 銀行純利鞘 → 地域中小企業の信用アクセス
